text:
WHOLE-BODY FDG PET/CT SCAN WITH DIAGNOSTIC CT OF NECK, CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS

PATIENT: Margaret Okonkwo
INDICATION: 52-year-old female with newly diagnosed HPV-positive (p16+) squamous cell carcinoma of the left base of tongue, pathologically confirmed by directed biopsy (moderately differentiated SCC, p16 strongly positive by IHC, high-risk HPV RNA ISH positive). Referred for whole-body FDG PET/CT for complete locoregional and systemic staging prior to initiation of definitive therapy. Clinical staging prior to this examination: T2 primary based on CT neck (2.2 cm left base of tongue mass), left cervical level IIA lymphadenopathy with central necrosis.

REFERRING PROVIDER: Head and Neck Oncology

TECHNIQUE: Whole-body FDG PET/CT was performed in accordance with departmental and SNMMI guidelines for oncologic FDG imaging. The patient fasted for a minimum of 6 hours prior to intravenous administration of 10.2 mCi (377 MBq) of F-18 fluorodeoxyglucose. Blood glucose at the time of injection was 89 mg/dL. Following a 60-minute uptake period in a quiet, low-light room with the patient at rest, emission data were acquired from skull base to mid-thigh in 3D mode. Concomitant low-dose CT for attenuation correction and anatomic localization was performed. Additionally, a separate diagnostic-quality CT scan of the neck with IV contrast (Omnipaque 350, 100 mL, injected via antecubital IV) was obtained in the portal venous phase to provide optimal soft tissue characterization of the primary site and cervical lymph node stations. CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed with IV contrast for assessment of distant disease. Images were reviewed on dedicated PET/CT workstation with simultaneous display of fused PET/CT, PET-only, and CT-only datasets in all three planes. SUVmax values are reported using lean body mass correction.

COMPARISON: CT neck with contrast (obtained at otolaryngology workup, approximately 5 weeks prior).

---

FINDINGS:

HEAD AND NECK - PRIMARY SITE:
There is an intensely hypermetabolic soft tissue mass involving the left base of tongue with extension toward the left vallecula. On the diagnostic CT component, the lesion measures approximately 2.2 cm in greatest dimension and demonstrates avid heterogeneous enhancement. The deep margin approaches but does not appear to cross the midline. The lesion does not extend to involve the epiglottis, the floor of mouth, or the lingual tonsil on the contralateral side. The overlying mucosa appears intact. FDG uptake within this lesion is markedly elevated, with an SUVmax of 14.2, substantially exceeding background soft tissue and salivary gland activity. The high glycolytic activity is consistent with viable, aggressive squamous cell carcinoma. There is no definitive involvement of the pre-epiglottic fat or the vallecula proper based on the CT component.

The right base of tongue, right tonsil, soft palate, posterior pharyngeal wall, supraglottic larynx, and hypopharynx are unremarkable. No additional FDG-avid mucosal lesion is identified to suggest a synchronous primary oropharyngeal malignancy. The nasopharynx appears normal without evidence of an additional primary.

HEAD AND NECK - LYMPH NODE STATIONS:
Left neck: There is a single dominant, markedly hypermetabolic lymph node in the left level IIA region (jugulodigastric group), measuring approximately 2.6 cm in short-axis diameter. The node demonstrates heterogeneous FDG uptake with an SUVmax of 11.8. On the diagnostic CT component, the nodal architecture is replaced by centrally hypodense, non-enhancing material consistent with necrosis, surrounded by a rim of peripheral enhancement. This pattern is highly characteristic of metastatic squamous cell carcinoma with central necrotic breakdown. A central photopenic region on the PET images corresponds precisely to the CT-defined zone of necrosis. One to two additional smaller lymph nodes in the left level II station measure up to 0.9 cm and demonstrate borderline FDG activity (SUVmax approximately 2.4), which is indeterminate and may reflect reactive adenopathy versus microscopic metastatic involvement; these nodes do not meet size criteria for definitive nodal metastasis. Left levels III, IV, and V are unremarkable. No left level IB, IA, retropharyngeal, or parapharyngeal nodal FDG uptake is identified.

Right neck: No hypermetabolic lymph nodes identified. No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes by size criteria. The right jugulodigastric, submandibular, and posterior cervical chain nodes are not FDG-avid.

Bilateral retropharyngeal nodes: No abnormal FDG uptake or CT-evident pathologically enlarged nodes in the retropharyngeal or parapharyngeal spaces bilaterally.

Supraclavicular and infraclavicular regions: No pathologically enlarged or FDG-avid lymph nodes bilaterally.

SALIVARY GLANDS:
The parotid glands are symmetric, with physiologic background FDG uptake. No focal hypermetabolic lesion. The bilateral submandibular glands demonstrate physiologic symmetric mild FDG activity. The sublingual and minor salivary glands are unremarkable.

ORBITS, SKULL BASE, AND CRANIUM:
No hypermetabolic intracranial lesions identified. The calvarium and skull base are intact without evidence of lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions on the CT component. Brain parenchyma is not routinely evaluated on whole-body FDG PET/CT due to high physiologic cerebral glucose metabolism; dedicated brain MRI is recommended if intracranial involvement is clinically suspected.

THYROID GLAND:
The thyroid gland is unremarkable in size and morphology. No focal hypermetabolic thyroid nodule identified. Background thyroid gland FDG uptake is within physiologic range.

CHEST:
LUNGS AND PLEURA: The lungs are clear. No pulmonary nodules, masses, or consolidation identified on the diagnostic CT component. No pleural effusion or pleural thickening. No FDG-avid pulmonary lesion.

MEDIASTINUM: The mediastinal contours are normal. No pathologically enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes are identified. No FDG-avid mediastinal nodal disease. The anterior, middle, and posterior mediastinal compartments are unremarkable.

HILAR REGIONS: Bilateral hila are symmetric and unremarkable. No hilar lymphadenopathy.

HEART AND PERICARDIUM: Cardiac silhouette is normal in size. No pericardial effusion. Physiologic FDG uptake in the myocardium.

BONY THORAX: The visualized ribs, sternum, thoracic spine, and bilateral clavicles demonstrate normal bone density without lytic or blastic lesions. No focal hypermetabolic osseous lesion identified.

ABDOMEN AND PELVIS:
LIVER: The liver is normal in size and morphology. No focal hepatic lesions on CT. No hepatic FDG uptake beyond physiologic background. No evidence of hepatic metastases.

SPLEEN: Normal in size and homogeneous. No focal FDG-avid lesion.

PANCREAS: Unremarkable. No focal pancreatic lesion.

ADRENAL GLANDS: Bilateral adrenal glands are normal in size and morphology without focal masses or FDG uptake exceeding background.

KIDNEYS: Bilateral kidneys are symmetric with normal parenchymal thickness. Physiologic FDG activity within the collecting systems from urinary excretion. No renal masses or hydronephrosis.

GASTROINTESTINAL TRACT: Normal-caliber loops of small and large bowel. No focal wall thickening or FDG-avid bowel lesion. Physiologic colonic FDG uptake.

PELVIS: The uterus and ovaries are unremarkable. No pelvic lymphadenopathy. No pelvic FDG-avid lesion identified.

PERITONEUM AND RETROPERITONEUM: No peritoneal implants. No retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. No ascites.

BONES (WHOLE BODY): No hypermetabolic osseous lesions identified throughout the imaged axial and appendicular skeleton. No lytic or blastic lesions to suggest osseous metastatic disease. Mild degenerative changes in the cervical and lumbar spine.

SKIN AND SOFT TISSUES: No hypermetabolic subcutaneous or soft tissue lesion identified.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Intensely hypermetabolic left base of tongue mass (SUVmax 14.2), measuring approximately 2.2 cm, consistent with the known primary squamous cell carcinoma. No CT or PET evidence of extension across the midline or involvement of adjacent structures beyond the base of tongue and vallecula.

2. Single markedly hypermetabolic left level IIA (jugulodigastric) lymph node, 2.6 cm, with central necrosis on CT and corresponding central photopenia on PET (SUVmax 11.8 in the peripheral viable rim), consistent with metastatic nodal involvement with necrosis. This represents the sole site of nodal metastasis identified.

3. One to two smaller left level II nodes with borderline FDG activity are indeterminate; clinical and radiologic correlation is recommended, and these may warrant inclusion in the radiation treatment planning volumes.

4. No contralateral cervical nodal involvement. No retropharyngeal, supraclavicular, mediastinal, hilar, or other regional nodal hypermetabolism identified.

5. No FDG-avid or CT-evident distant metastatic disease in the chest, abdomen, or pelvis.

6. No synchronous FDG-avid primary malignancy identified.

Overall, findings are consistent with locoregional disease confined to the primary left base of tongue site and a single left level IIA metastatic lymph node, with no evidence of distant metastatic spread. These findings support a locoregional stage consistent with T2N1 per clinical and radiologic assessment in the context of HPV-positive oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma.

Interpreted by: Attending Nuclear Medicine / Diagnostic Radiology
Clinical service: Head and Neck Oncology
